etymology.description: semantic 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 尞(láo) -> flooded
etymology.interpretation: 潦 combines the semantic component 水(水) (shuǐ, "water"), indicating involvement with water or liquid, with the phonetic component 尞(láo), which provides the sound. This structure suggests a meaning related to water, specifically 'flooded,' as it involves an excess or overflow of water.

examples.chinese: 大雨之后，村庄被潦水淹没(大雨之後，村莊被潦水淹沒)。
examples.pinyin: Dà yǔ zhīhòu, cūnzhuāng bèi liáo shuǐ yānmò.
examples.english: After the heavy rain, the village was submerged by floodwaters.

examples.chinese: 街道上的水涨到了脚踝(街道上的水漲到了腳踝)，看来是被潦所困(看來是被潦所困)。
examples.pinyin: Jiēdào shàng de shuǐ zhǎng dào le jiǎohuái, kàn lái shì bèi liáo suǒ kùn.
examples.english: The water on the street rose to the ankles, seemingly trapped by the flood.

examples.chinese: 潦水退去后(潦水退去後)，才可以开始清理(才可以開始清理)。
examples.pinyin: Liáo shuǐ tuì qù hòu, cái kěyǐ kāishǐ qīnglǐ.
examples.english: Only after the floodwaters recede can we begin cleaning up.
